title: API Response Time Monitoring (ThousandEyes)
dataSources: `index=thousandeyes`, ThousandEyes OTel Tests Stream вАФ Metrics (API tests)
spl:
`stream_index` thousandeyes.test.type="api"
| timechart span=5m avg(api.duration) as avg_api_duration_s by thousandeyes.test.name
description: Tracks total API test execution duration including all steps, revealing when API performance degrades from the consumer's perspective.
value: Tracks total API test execution duration including all steps, revealing when API performance degrades from the consumer's perspective.
implementation: The OTel metric `api.duration` reports total API test execution time in seconds. For per-step analysis, use `api.step.duration` filtered by `thousandeyes.test.step`. The Splunk App Application dashboard includes an "API Request Duration (s)" line chart with permalink drilldown.
visualization: Line chart (API duration over time), Table (test, duration), Column chart (duration by step).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
вАҐ Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Cisco ThousandEyes App for Splunk` (Splunkbase 7719).
вАҐ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=thousandeyes`, ThousandEyes OTel Tests Stream вАФ Metrics (API tests).
вАҐ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 вАФ Configure data collection
The OTel metric `api.duration` reports total API test execution time in seconds. For per-step analysis, use `api.step.duration` filtered by `thousandeyes.test.step`. The Splunk App Application dashboard includes an "API Request Duration (s)" line chart with permalink drilldown.

Step 2 вАФ Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
`stream_index` thousandeyes.test.type="api"
| timechart span=5m avg(api.duration) as avg_api_duration_s by thousandeyes.test.name
```

Understanding this SPL

**API Response Time Monitoring (ThousandEyes)** вАФ Tracks total API test execution duration including all steps, revealing when API performance degrades from the consumer's perspective.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=thousandeyes`, ThousandEyes OTel Tests Stream вАФ Metrics (API tests). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Cisco ThousandEyes App for Splunk` (Splunkbase 7719). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feedвАФrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

вАҐ Invokes macro `stream_index` вАФ in Search, use the UI or expand to inspect the underlying SPL.
вАҐ `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=5m** buckets with a separate series **by thousandeyes.test.name** вАФ ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.


Step 3 вАФ Validate
Compare the same tests and time window in the Cisco ThousandEyes App for Splunk dashboard or the test view at app.thousandeyes.com so SplunkвАЩs metrics and states match the vendor. If they disagree, check streaming or HEC inputs, macros, and API or token health before retuning.

Step 4 вАФ Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (API duration over time), Table (test, duration), Column chart (duration by step).
grandmaExplanation: We time API responses from the same perspective users get, so backend delay versus network delay is easier to untangle.
knownFalsePositives: API response time lags from gateway queues, token refresh storms, and dependency timeouts masked as network delay.
